transcript.title: Opening
transcript.text: Good morning Rushil Bhatia. Here is your daily news for Tuesday, July 21. In today's briefing: top headlines, bhopal and madhya pradesh, business and markets, money, tax and personal finance, science and technology, development and policy, across india, around the world. Let's begin.

transcript.title: Top headlines
transcript.text: First, the stories leading the news this morning. Trump imposes 50 percent tariffs on most Canadian goods, cites trade disputes. President Trump imposed fifty percent tariffs on most Canadian goods. This action escalates trade tensions between the two nations. The tariffs apply to many goods previously protected under the USMCA agreement. This move heightens economic uncertainty and strains bilateral ties. 'They will pay many times over': Trump as US carries out 10th night of Iran strikes. United States forces conducted tenth night of strikes against Iran. These operations aim to reopen the Strait of Hormuz after recent attacks. Iran also targeted US allies Kuwait, Jordan, and Bahrain with missile barrages. Diplomatic efforts continue while the interim ceasefire agreement has collapsed. Messi suffers unwanted first in FIFA World Cup final as Spain silence Argentina icon. Spain defeated Argentina in the FIFA World Cup 2026 final after extra time. Lionel Messi experienced a rare off day, failing to create any chances for teammates. Spain's defensive strategy successfully contained the legendary Argentine forward throughout the match. Ferran Torres scored the decisive goal, securing Spain's second World Cup title. Day 1 of Parliament's monsoon session washed out amid NEET, donation chori protests. The first day of Parliament's monsoon session was disrupted by opposition protests. Opposition parties raised concerns over various issues, including temple donations and exam leaks. Frequent adjournments led to the cancellation of proceedings in both houses. A bill to increase the number of Supreme Court judges was introduced in Lok Sabha. Engineers building Solar panels are using exact replicas of honeycombs made by Bees to ensure . Engineers have developed new solar panels inspired by bee honeycombs. This innovative design significantly improves sunlight absorption and power generation. The honeycomb structure traps light, preventing reflection and increasing energy capture. Tests show substantial power output gains compared to traditional flat panels. Andy Burnham pledges to end rough sleeping in Britain in his first speech as new UK PM. Andy Burnham has become the United Kingdom's seventh prime minister in ten years. He made his first pledge to end rough sleeping across the country. Burnham met the king at Buckingham Palace before heading to Downing Street. He promised to address the cost of living and build more council homes. Indus Water Treaty in present form no longer exists, Pak aiding terrorism: India. The Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) with Pakistan will no longer function in its present form, the government has concluded hardening its position on the contentious water issue amid little indication of Islamabad discontinuing its covert backing of cross-border terrorism. Defence ministry seeks firms for rupees 15,000 cr high-altitude airship project. India's defence ministry seeks domestic firms for airship-based pseudo satellites. These platforms will provide persistent intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance capabilities. The program aims to offer lower-cost alternatives to existing satellite systems. Airships will hover in the stratosphere, bridging gaps between drones and satellites. Florida returned 500,000 oyster shells to Gulf, creating new reef habitat. Florida restored parts of the Gulf of Mexico by returning more than 500,000 tonnes of recycled oyster and clam shells to the sea between 2007 and 2024. The shells helped rebuild oyster reefs, improve water quality and support the return of fish, turtles and dolphins to levels not seen in over 25 years. Big Bear's famous eagle chicks need $10M habitat rescue by July 31. Thousands of people around the world are watching bald eagles Jackie and Shadow raise their two eaglets through a live nest camera in California's Big Bear Valley. As the chicks continue to grow, conservationists are also racing to raise $10 million to protect the surrounding habitat from development, highlighting the importance of preserving the forests and open spaces the eagles need to survive. Vacant for decades? Why the government can't simply take over abandoned properties. In India, the absence of a comprehensive law governing abandoned buildings leads to fragmented regulations across various legislative acts. Municipal authorities hold the authority to inspect and classify buildings as dangerous. Owners are notified to undertake necessary repairs or secure their properties, with demolition a potential option in cases of imminent risk. Both Maharashtra and Delhi enforce mandatory structural evaluations for older buildings. Supreme Court seeks more exclusive UAPA, NDPS courts to speed up trials. NEW DELHI: To address the grievance of accused facing long years of incarceration in UAPA and NDPS cases, SC Monday pushed for the establishment of additional exclusive special courts to speed up trials in NIA-investigated cases and those related to narcotic drugs registered by various police organisations.

transcript.title: Bhopal and Madhya Pradesh
transcript.text: Now, news closer to home in Bhopal and across Madhya Pradesh. CM Mohan Yadav Asks Police To Stay Alert On Farmers' Issues, Student Protests; Announces 9,000 Recruitments. Chief Minister Mohan Yadav on Monday directed the Madhya Pradesh Police to stay prepared for issues related to farmers, including fertiliser shortages and drought-like conditions, besides remaining alert to student protests. MP Government Tables Medical University Amendment Bill Among 14 Bills In Assembly. The Madhya Pradesh government on Monday introduced a Bill in the Assembly to establish the Madhya Pradesh Dhanvantri Health University in Ujjain. Encroachments, E-Rickshaws Slow Bhopal's First Electric Bus Trial. The first trial of Bhopal's electric buses under the PM eBus Sewa scheme exposed persistent traffic bottlenecks caused by roadside encroachments and unregulated e-rickshaws in the old city. Doctor Alleges rupees 20 Lakh Extortion Threatened With False Rape Case In Bhopal. Habibganj police have registered a case against a woman after a government doctor alleged she extorted nearly rupees 20 lakh by threatening to implicate him in a false rape case. Farmers, Karni Sena Stopped On Way To Bhopal Over Ring Road Compensation Demand. Tension gripped Shipra Square after police stopped farmers and Karni Sena Parivar members marching to Bhopal over compensation for land acquired for the Indore-Dewas Western Ring Road project. A journey of hope and transformation for women and children in Madhya Pradesh. SC gives Centre 4-weeks to confer gallantry award on former Madhya Pradesh cop. 19-Year-Old Arrested For Uncle's Murder In Indore; Property Dispute, Drug Addiction Suspected. Police have arrested a 19-year-old youth for allegedly murdering his 50-year-old uncle in Indore's Chandan Nagar area. Rain, lighting alert for Bhopal till July 23. Madhya Pradesh Assembly Introduces Uniform Civil Code Bill, 2026. 38 Years Later, High Court Awards Relief For 200 Trees. The Indore bench of the Madhya Pradesh High Court has directed the State government to pay rupees 50,000 with statutory interest as compensation for 200 Babool trees acquired for the Hari Phatak overbridge project in 1988. Confusing signboards add to mounting Metro traffic woes. Bhopal Police to hold 'Run For Life' marathon against drug abuse. NGT asks for identification, demarcation of buffer zone at Shahpura Lake. Man killed in hit-and-run. Government assures upgrade of Maha's MP Bhavan for Warkaris from state. Tractor rally towards Bhopal: Water cannon, tear gas used against protesting farmers. Sandipani schools to be inaugurated before Guru Purnima: CM. Bill proposing varsity courses from high-rises introduced in Assembly. Madhya Pradesh cabinet approves Uniform Civil Code.

transcript.title: Business and markets
transcript.text: Moving to business, the economy and the markets. India's demat boom hits a trading slowdown as retail investors step away. Active trading accounts fell to 19.6 percent of India's 23.2 crore demat accounts in Q1 FY27, down from 26.3 percent in FY24, as weaker returns, Sebi's derivative curbs and Iran-war volatility pushed retail investors to the sidelines. Paytm Q1 Results: Net profit jumps 79 percent , revenue up 28 percent ; board opts not to proceed with bonus shares. One97 Communications reported a 28 percent YoY revenue increase to rupees 2,448 crore for Q1 FY27, driven by strong growth in Payment Services. Net profit surged 79 percent YoY to rupees 220 crore, reflecting improved operating efficiency and growth in financial services. BlueStone Jewellery shares to be in focus on Tuesday as company turns profitable in Q1, revenue jumps 49 percent. BlueStone Jewellery reported a strong Q1FY27, with a 49 percent YoY increase in retail sales to rupees 733 crore. The company also saw a 39 percent growth in same-store sales despite higher gold customs duties, and an improved EBITDA margin, reflecting strong operating leverage. Gold, silver prices today: Comex gold and silver trade steady as geopolitical tensions fuel rate worries. Gold and silver prices remained volatile amid escalating US-Iran clashes and renewed diplomatic efforts. Inflation concerns and higher interest rates may limit precious metals' upside despite geopolitical uncertainty supporting safe-haven demand. Wall Street: S and P 500, Nasdaq rebound as chipmakers recover, investors brace for big tech earnings. As of 10:15 a.m. Eastern Time, the S and P 500 rose 0.3 percent , the Dow Jones Industrial Average was down 0.2 percent , and the Nasdaq Composite was 0.5 percent higher. US stocks to buy for short term: From Nvidia to Netflix- Appreciate CEO suggests picking these 5 shares. Appreciate CEO Subho Moulik reveals five key stocks to watch. From Nvidia's AI dominance to Netflix's innovative revenue strategies, these companies showcase how the evolving landscape of technology is shaping investment opportunities. Suzlon Energy board to consider Q1FY27 results on July 28; shares down 10 percent in July. Suzlon Energy's Board of Directors will meet on July 28 to approve Q1FY27 financial results. The company reported a 5.6 percent decline in consolidated net profit for March 2026, despite rising operational revenue. Expert view: Overweight on diversified financials, automobiles, new age tech firms, says Nandish Shah of Motilal Oswal. Expert view: Nandish Shah, AVP-PCG Research and Advisory, (Fundamental) Wealth Management, Motilal Oswal Financial Services, is overweight on diversified financials, automobiles, new age tech platforms, manufacturing and industrials, and consumer discretionary sectors. Iran-US deal: Mediators propose 10-day ceasefire. Senior Iranian officials have revealed to Reuters that mediators are pushing for a 10-day ceasefire. US stock market today: Wall Street futures gain as easing oil prices offset lingering Middle East tensions. US stock futures rose on 20 July as oil prices fell amid renewed US-Iran diplomatic efforts, easing supply concerns. The S and P 500, Nasdaq-100, and Dow Jones futures gained, though geopolitical tensions in the Middle East persisted, affecting global markets and chip stocks. Banks draw thinner margins on tepid credit growth. Top private sector banks reported weaker net interest margins (NIMs) in the June quarter as sluggish retail loan demand pushed them towards lower-yielding corporate loans. Aastha Spintex sets board meeting date to consider and approve 1:1 bonus shares, 100 percent final dividend. Aastha Spintex's board will meet on July 23 to discuss a bonus issue and a final dividend for shareholders. The proposed bonus involves issuing new shares and reflects confidence in the company's growth following an acquisition to expand spinning capacity. Godrej Agrovet bets big on oil palm push. Edible oil prices are expected to stay elevated for several months ahead. Sobha Q1 Results: Revenue rises 50 percent , profit more than triples as margins improve. Sobha Ltd. reported a strong June 2026 quarter with a 50 percent year-on-year revenue rise to rupees 1,278.2 crore and a net profit tripling to rupees 50.8 crore. Despite a 35.7 percent sequential revenue decline, operating margins improved significantly. CJP protest: More than 50 cops injured in clashes. Over fifty police personnel sustained injuries during clashes with CJP protesters in New Delhi. Top Gainers and Losers on 20 July: Axis Bank, HDFC Bank, Ceat, India Cements, OLA, HFCL among top losers. The Indian stock market faced pressure on 20 July as geopolitical tensions in the Middle East weighed on investor sentiment. Rising crude prices stoked fears of tighter monetary policies, while mixed sector performances were seen. Despite losses, heavyweight stocks helped Nifty 50 end down 0.40 percent . Judge halts Paramount–Warner Bros deal. California and eleven states secured a pause on Paramount's acquisition of Warner Bros. Indian Overseas Bank share price rise 3 percent after Q1 results 2026. Do you own?. IOB share price opened at rupees 34.14 apiece today, as compared to previous close of rupees 33.81 on Friday last week. 27 trapped in Sikkim tunnel landslide. Twenty-seven workers are trapped inside a Sikkim tunnel after a landslide blocked its entrance. No proposal under consideration to scrap LTCG tax on equities, government clarifies. The central government clarifies that it has no plans to abolish the long-term capital gains tax on equity transactions for retail and domestic investors, quashing market speculation. Details here. Bengali mandatory in Government offices from Sept 1. West Bengal will mandate Bengali for all government departments starting September 1. ISM 2.0: Can semiconductor stocks become India's next long-term wealth creators?. The Union Cabinet had approved rupees 1.27 lakh crore for the second edition of India Semiconductor Mission (ISM 2.0). At the current juncture, India offers an emerging manufacturing and infrastructure story backed by policy support. Bangladesh Army Chief in Turkiye for defence push. Bangladesh Army chief General Waker-Uz-Zaman is visiting Turkiye to enhance military training partnerships. PNB shares jump 5.5 percent to 2-month high after Q1 profit more than triples; brokerages turn bullish. Punjab National Bank shares surged 5.6 percent to rupees 111.70, driven by a 214 percent net profit increase to rupees 5,253 crore for Q1FY27, supported by stronger operations and improved asset quality, reflecting a favorable earnings outlook. 'Indus Water Treaty needs full renegotiation'. India states the Indus Waters Treaty needs renegotiation due to Pakistan's inaction. UltraTech Cement Q1 Results: Net profit jumps 17 percent YoY to rupees 2,599 crore. In Q1FY27, UltraTech Cement's net profit surged 16.77 percent year-on-year to rupees 2,599.28 crore, driven by operational efficiencies and market execution. Revenue grew 15.8 percent to rupees 24,648 crore, while capacity utilisation stood at 81 percent , indicating strong demand in the construction sector. Trump threatens Iran with heavy reprisals. US President Donald Trump warned Iran that it would pay "many times over" for every American soldier killed after Washington announced three new military fatalities and one service member missing over the weekend. Axis Bank share price dips over 6 percent after Q1FY27 results. Axis Bank shares fell nearly 6 percent due to concerns over net interest margins despite a strong profit growth. The bank reported a consolidated net profit increase of 22.23 percent for the June quarter, yet asset quality showed a slight decline in non-performing assets. US may ban Chinese AI models: Axios. The Trump administration is exploring ways to limit access to advanced Chinese AI models.

transcript.title: Money, tax and personal finance
transcript.text: Now to your money — taxes, savings and personal finance. Did F and O or intraday trading last year? Your ITR filing due date isn't July 31. In India, intraday and F and O trading profits are taxed differently. Intraday trading is speculative business income, while F and O is non-speculative. Taxpayers must file ITR-3 or ITR-4 depending on specific conditions. Here are the deadlines. EPFO 3.0: Government plans new pension scheme for formal and unorganised sector workers, claims report explaining how it will work. The government is planning a new pension scheme under the EPFO 3.0 framework. This new plan aims to provide retirement benefits to workers in both formal and unorganised sectors. Members will build a retirement corpus through regular contributions invested in government-backed assets. Claiming a deceased family member's mutual funds made less cumbersome: What SEBI has changed. SEBI has asked AMFI to simplify the industry's transmission norms, making it less cumbersome for nominees and legal heirs to claim rights on mutual funds after the investor's death. Here's what changed under the revised framework. How NRIs, OCIs can get 45 percent annual return on $1 lakh FCNR (B) deposit as this bank in India offers 19X leverage; Should you go for it?. HSBC Bank's IFSC unit offers Non-Resident Indians significant leverage on FCNR(B) deposits. This strategy allows investors to borrow up to nineteen times their initial deposit amount. The potential for amplified returns arises from the spread between deposit earnings and borrowing costs. However, this leverage strategy carries risks if borrowing costs exceed deposit interest rates. Aadhaar app crosses 4 crore downloads: Full list of services available on the new app. The new Aadhaar app achieved 40 million downloads so far. The redesigned app replaces mAadhaar aap and offers a wide range of Aadhaar-related services. Full list here. Buying gold? This simple COMEX calculation can tell if you are being overcharged. By converting international gold prices into Indian rates, you can find the actual gold cost in India and better understand whether your jewellery bill reflects a reasonable price. 8th Pay Commission salary hike: Will 2.0 fitment factor increase gross salary by just 31 percent ? Check pay calculator. Central government employees anticipate significant salary raises from pay commissions. However, gross salary increases may lag behind basic pay hikes. This difference arises from various allowances that do not always scale proportionally. Historical data shows gross salary hikes have been lower than basic pay increases. HDFC Balanced Advantage Fund versus Parag Parikh Dynamic Asset Allocation: Same category, but equity exposure 71 percent versus 15 percent. Balanced advantage funds can have vastly different portfolio structures despite belonging to the same SEBI category. Variations in equity, debt and cash can lead to distinct risk profiles and return outcomes, making portfolio composition an important factor to evaluate. EPS rules explained: Eligibility, withdrawal and pension. Confused about the Employees' Pension Scheme and its rules? This guide breaks down complex aspects, including contribution methods, eligibility, and pension calculations. With increasing cases of claim rejections, being informed is key. Job switch leads to rupees 1.69 lakh tax liability despite both employers deducting TDS correctly: Here's why Form 12B matters. Switching jobs during a financial year can leave salaried employees with an unexpected tax liability even if both employers deducted TDS correctly. The gap arises because TDS is calculated employer-wise, while final tax is based on total annual income. Form 12B can help avoid this mismatch. ITR filing 2026: Step-by-step guide to filing ITR-1 for AY 2026-27 before the July 31 deadline. Students, salaried individuals, and pensioners can file ITR-1 for AY 2026-2027. The deadline for filing this income tax return is July 31, 2026. This simplified form is for resident individuals with income up to fifty lakh rupees. It covers income from salary, one house property, and other specified sources. How to lock and unlock your fingerprints and iris using new Aadhaar App. The Aadhaar biometric lock/unlock feature has gained significant adoption, with residents using it more than 1.91 crore times, according to MeitY. This security feature allows Aadhaar holders to prevent unauthorized biometric authentication using their fingerprints, iris and face data. 7 big income tax changes for 2026-27 that may reduce penalties for small mistakes. New income tax rules aim to reduce penalties for ordinary citizens. Minor foreign asset non-disclosures will not face prosecution from October 2024. Prosecution provisions are softened with reduced jail terms and graded punishments. Several penalties are converted into fixed fees to reduce litigation and bring certainty. The NRI guilt trap: why high salaries abroad don't always create wealth. Many NRIs earn in dollars but struggle to build wealth as family obligations, emotional guilt and social expectations drain savings and delay financial independence. The coaching race is costing parents lakhs. Here's how to plan. Parents are spending rupees 2-4 lakh on coaching before college admissions. With education inflation at 10-12 percent , experts warn against dipping into retirement savings or taking on debt. rupees 1 lakh Investment turns into rupees 2.98 lakh: Sovereign Gold Bond 2021-22 Series premature redemption date today. The Reserve Bank of India has set the premature redemption price for SGB 2021-22 Series-IV. Investors can redeem these bonds prematurely from July 20, 2026, after five years. Tax rules apply to capital gains on premature redemption of these bonds. National Student Laptop Scheme 2026 is fake: PIB Fact Check reiterates warning against viral WhatsApp message. PIB Fact Check has again debunked a viral WhatsApp message, first flagged in January, claiming the Government is accepting applications for free laptops under the 'National Student Laptop Scheme 2026'. It reiterated that no such scheme exists. ITR-4 or ITR-3? Know which ITR form to file under presumptive taxation scheme and the documents required. Taxpayers using the presumptive taxation scheme must select the correct Income Tax Return form. Generally, ITR-4 is suitable for individuals and HUFs with income up to fifty lakh rupees. However, certain conditions like foreign income or losses necessitate filing ITR-3. Essential documents include PAN, Aadhaar, bank details, and tax payment proofs. Hotels can't add a service charge to your bill without informing you: CCPA acts against 41 restaurants. CCPA has initiated action against 41 restaurants for automatically adding service charges to customers' bills, calling it an unfair trade practice. The move reinforces that service charges are voluntary, and restaurants must not levy them by default or without clearly informing consumers. Salary has to be paid before 7th day of the succeeding month under new labour code; Know what else the new labour code says about salary timeline. Under the new labour code, employers must pay salaries by the seventh day of the following month. Daily wage workers must be paid at the end of each workday. Employees who leave a job are entitled to receive their final dues within two working days. The code applies to all employees, regardless of salary, and introduces the role of an inspector-cum-facilitator to help resolve wage-related complaints and claims. EPF interest rate: 8.25 percent interest credited? Here's how to check your PF balance. EPFO members were expected to receive 8.25 percent interest on their EPF contributions for FY 2025-26 on July 15, 2026. The Future of Insurance in India Sarbvir Singh, Joint Group CEO, PB Fintech. India's insurance industry is changing faster than ever. Gift tax rules: Cash, property, jewellery and shares; Here's when you'll pay tax and report it in your ITR. Gifts received from specified relatives are fully exempt from income tax, regardless of their value. 7 things to know about a retirement income tool that most Indians overlook and it's not for retirees only!. An annuity is a contract with an insurance company. Government notifies new Income Tax Rules 2026 for stricter compliance. Opinion: GST on laptops is a tax on education. Income Tax Slab for Senior Citizens Above 60 Years for AY 2026-27: With Deductions, Rebates and Examples.

transcript.title: Science and technology
transcript.text: Next, science and technology. Here are the 30,000 songs Sony is suing Udio's AI music generator over. Sony Music Entertainment has filed another lawsuit against Udio, accusing the AI music generator of infringing the copyright of more than 30,000 of its songs, ranging from Elvis Presley's Hound Dog to Beyoncé's Say My Name, and Harry Styles' As It Was. The lawsuit, filed in a New York court on Monday, claims that this Colossal Biosciences reportedly in talks to raise new capital at $20B–$30B valuation. The de-extinction startup is looking to double or triple its previous valuation, according to the report. Some dinosaurs could rise like giants until they grew too big. Two elephant-sized South American sauropods were unusually well built for standing on their hind legs. Digital tests showed that their robust femurs handled the enormous forces better than those of larger species, especially while the dinosaurs were young. The upright pose may have helped them reach treetops, frighten predators, or impress potential mates. The FCC is planning to retroactively ban disguised DJI gadgets. Last October, we told you how the FCC had given itself the power to retroactively ban gadgets that have already received its approval to be imported and sold in the United States. Now, the FCC's getting ready to wield that power for the first time, by cracking down on the "DJI front companies" suspected of Trump's latest AI czar has already resigned. The director role for the Center for AI Standards and Innovation (CAISI) has become a revolving door since David Sacks left his position as czar. The Odyssey turned me into an IMAX believer. After seeing Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey for the first time early last week, I came away impressed, but somewhat conflicted about two of the film's more fantastical set pieces. While those scenes were beautifully crafted and tremendously acted, there was something a little bit off about the way they hit my eye. I spent days Google is working on a new AI chip designed to make Gemini more efficient. Alphabet, Google's parent company, is reportedly working on a new chip designed to make its Gemini models run much more efficiently. An ordinary laptop solved a problem thought to require a quantum computer. A quantum problem once described as impossible for classical computers has now been solved using relatively modest hardware. Researchers used tensor networks to compress the overwhelming wave function created by hundreds of entangled qubits, allowing some calculations to run on a laptop. Their results matched both theoretical predictions and simulations performed with a quantum computer. The method could open new paths for exploring quantum dynamics and materials. SpaceX in your index fund, explained. Index funds are touted as one of the safest ways to invest. Rather than picking and choosing individual stocks, index funds let you bet on the market as a whole. So what happens when a company like SpaceX - a giant gamble, and, in my opinion, terribly overpriced - is fast-tracked into the Nasdaq-100? Does AI's most important protocol is getting a little bit easier to use. Under the new system, the protocol will take a looser, "stateless" approach to session IDs on the server side, similar to how most ordinary websites already work. Earth's waters are quietly running out of oxygen, scientists warn. Oxygen is disappearing from oceans, lakes, rivers, and coastal waters at an alarming rate, threatening aquatic life and weakening the natural processes that help regulate Earth's climate. Scientists warn that this widespread deoxygenation is closely connected to other major planetary threats, including warming, pollution, biodiversity loss, and ocean acidification. Get Borderlands 3, Risk of Rain 2 and 13 other great PC games for $15. The aptly-named "2K Megahits 2026 Bundle" from Humble includes 15 Steam games for $15. It's a smattering of the publisher 2K's biggest games of the 2010s, including BioShock Infinite, Risk of Rain 2, Borderlands 3, and XCOM: Enemy Unknown. The bundle will be available until August 7th, though the bundle page notes that keys for X relaunches a rebuilt Android app after year-long effort. X says the rebuilt version of its Android app is now available globally. Alzheimer's breakthrough: Scientists restore two hours of sleep without clearing brain plaques. Researchers have uncovered a surprising culprit behind sleep loss in Alzheimer's disease: the brain's own immune cells. In mice with amyloid plaques, overactive microglia triggered inflammation that kept the brain from getting enough deep, restorative sleep. Temporarily removing most of these cells restored more than two hours of sleep per day, even though the plaques remained unchanged. The PlayStation replica ornament is an homage to a great, yet fragile console. You probably know the signature PlayStation boot sound. Did you know that it's technically a multi-part chime? There's the synthy section where "Sony Computer Entertainment" shows onscreen with a white background. You only get to the next screen with the echo-y chimes and the color-filled PlayStation logo if your console recognizes the disc. OpenAI is scared of open-weight models. Should the US be?. Talk of banning Chinese-made open-weight LLMs reveals the challenge of turning AI into a business. Ford's $30,000 electric truck: all the news about the company's big EV re-do. The end of the Ford F-150 Lightning was also the start of a new era for the automaker. After failing to capture the market with the electric version of its popular F-Series truck, Ford is going back to the drawing board with its Universal Electric Vehicle Platform. The first offering will be a $30,000 electric Natural raises $30M to reinvent payments for AI agents , and take on Stripe. The one-year-old startup aims to reinvent financial architecture for autonomous AI transactions. UCLA scientists develop mineral sunscreen without the chalky white cast. Scientists have redesigned zinc oxide particles to create a mineral sunscreen that protects against ultraviolet radiation without leaving such a noticeable white cast. The SPF 30 formula could make sunscreen easier and more appealing to use across a wider range of skin tones. Judge pauses Paramount's attempt to buy Warner Bros. Discovery. A judge partially granted the request from a dozen state attorneys general to temporarily place the $110 billion merger of Paramount and Warner Bros. Discovery on hold, as reported by Variety and Reuters. US District Judge Araceli Martínez-Olguín said that based on the new company's market share, "the Court is persuaded that it can presume Judge pauses $110B Paramount-Warner Bros. merger. The lawsuit from the states alleges that the deal would harm movie theaters, basic cable distributors, and audiences. LG's glossy OLED gaming monitor is rare to find under $400. If you've been thinking about upgrading your gaming monitor, LG's 27-inch 27GX704A-B pairs a glossy WOLED panel with a fast refresh rate, and it's currently on sale for $379.99 (about $70 off) at Amazon and directly from LG, marking a new low price. It originally launched at $799.99, but has been available for under $500 since Adobe camera app's new feature will critique your photos using AI. Adobe's Project Indigo can now remove all kinds of backgrounds from photos you snap using the app. LG's monitors come with an unwanted addition for Windows: McAfee pop-up ads. A video from Gamers Nexus explains how, after connecting a new LG UltraGear monitor to a PC running Windows 11 for the first time, Windows Update is silently installing LG driver updates and the LG Monitor App Installer, without so much as a permission pop-up or notification. The app doesn't appear to include special controls Hackers are exploiting recently patched WordPress bugs, putting millions of websites at risk. Two critical security flaws in WordPress' software have given hackers the chance to remotely take over tens of millions of websites, according to an estimate by a cybersecurity researcher. YouTube clarifies policies around AI slop and upsetting videos. YouTube has updated its monetization policies to more clearly define the kinds of AI-generated and low-quality videos that can't earn ad revenue.

transcript.title: Development and policy
transcript.text: Now to development, infrastructure and government policy. Tourism and infrastructure threaten habitat of India's Himalayan salamanders. India-Seychelles Relations: Exploring Blue Economy Opportunities. "I think the water is a little overdone... the actual water requirements are really not that high" says Kuldeep Jain, MD of CleanMax Enviro Energy Solutions, on why power, not water or land, is the biggest constraint for AI data centres. Speaking to Mangalam Mal. BJP describes govt's E20 as one of India's biggest public policy successes. India's infrastructure output rises 5 percent y/y in June under new series. Egis appoints Vipul Surana as Chief Executive Officer for India. 'Will not be functional in present form': India firm on Indus Waters Treaty until Pakistan ends cross-border terrorism. India says allegedly leaked nuclear plant files pose no safety risk. RITES, MECON sign MoU to jointly pursue infrastructure projects in India and overseas. How India can build stronger manufacturing economy. India's trade policy review at WTO slated for July 21 and 23.

transcript.title: Across India
transcript.text: Turning to the big national stories. J and K floods: Lal Chowk flooded, Amarnath yatra stays suspended. Heavy rainfall inundated Srinagar's Lal Chowk and other city areas on Monday morning. Netizens criticized the government over the drainage system's alleged failure. Officials cited overwhelming rainfall, while critics questioned the Smart City infrastructure project. The Omar Abdullah cabinet previously raised concerns and ordered an external audit. NC's Delhi protest draws oppn flak; BJP slams Omar for ignoring floods, PDP asks him to emulate Wangchuk. National Conference held a protest in Delhi seeking statehood restoration. Other parties criticized the party president and chief minister for their absence. BJP stated the government should have been present to manage flood relief efforts. PDP suggested learning from activist Sonam Wangchuk's mobilization efforts. Global urea prices ease after May spike, fall 40 percent in June. Urea prices fell significantly in June, offering government relief after earlier increases. International urea prices were $572 per tonne, down from May's figures. Other fertilizer prices like diammonium phosphate and potash remained higher than last year. Sulphur prices saw the largest increase, rising 265 percent year-on-year. Delhi violence not spontaneous, says BJP, blames Left. The BJP has blamed Left-affiliated student groups for protests and violence. Organised groups were also accused of instigating and mobilising crowds. Similar allegations were made regarding the Delhi riots and ongoing legal proceedings. The party stated that the violence did not appear spontaneous and was orchestrated. J and K flood toll climbs to 28 as fresh deaths hit Poonch, Doda; rescuers dig through debris as rain threat persists. Torrential rain caused flash floods and landslides, killing twenty-eight people across Jammu division. Poonch reported twenty-three deaths, including several children, after a house collapse. Rescuers dug four bodies from rubble left by raging streams and collapsing hillsides. Amarnath Yatra and pilgrimages to Vaishno Devi shrine remained suspended for a second day. "Modi Government has become extremely arrogant": Arvind Kejriwal on Lathi-Charge against protesters. AAP national convener Arvind Kejriwal on Monday launched a scathing attack on the Centre over the police action against students, calling the Modi government "extremely arrogant" for using force against peaceful protesters. 'Power does not last forever': Rahul Gandhi's warning to police after tear gas, lathicharge on student protesters. NEW DELHI: Congress MP and Leader of Opposition in the Lok Sabha Rahul Gandhi on Monday warned police officers that 'power does not last forever' and 'accountability will certainly be enforced', saying the Constitution, not the government is their 'master'. CJP march: Police say detained protesters 'not students'; more arrests likely. NEW DELHI: Delhi Police on Monday said the protesters detained during the Cockroach Janata Party's march to Parliament were not students and that their identities were being verified. India, Moldova discuss UNSC reforms, fight against terror. The President held bilateral discussions with her Moldovan counterpart Maia Sandu. Fake snakebite deaths, forged autopsies: Chhattisgarh probe leads to doctor's arrest. Investigators claimed forged post-mortem reports were prepared, falsely showing snakebite as the cause of death in several cases. Indus Waters Treaty: Government focus on Chenab projects, renegotiation. The Indian Express has learnt that Delhi's key priority is the speedy completion of hydropower projects on the Chenab. 2 IPS trainees, harassment complaint, 'suicide' attempt: Case that rocked Hyderabad police academy. Constable who quit his job and cleared UPSC is now in hospital, a day after he was booked in a sexual harassment case 6 months, a plan to kill and an AI tool: What police found in Bengaluru triple murder. The alleged motive, according to case records: a dispute over money Shwetha had taken from her parents to spend on her partner. Stone pelted on India's first hydrogen train? Indian Railways responds to viral video. A viral video claimed India's first hydrogen train was targeted with stone pelting. Russia-Ukraine war: 4 Indian sailors among 10 killed as missile hits ship. According to the Ukrainian Air Force, Russian forces launched three Kh-59/Kh-69 cruise missiles during the attack. 'He was set to get married, change jobs': 4 Indians killed in Russian attack on cargo ship off Ukraine coast. As per the Ministry of External Affairs, on the evening of July 19, the vessel was attacked while departing the port of Odesa Magisterial probe ordered into killing of youth in J and K's Doda. Police claimed three other policemen were also injured in the attack. Assam floods disrupt rail services: Over 30 trains cancelled, diverted and short-terminated – Full list. Assam flood has disrupted rail traffic, with over 30 trains cancelled, diverted or short-terminated. No Detailed Project Report received for metro rail extension upto Rishikesh: Centre. The Centre has said it has not received a Detailed Project Report (DPR) for the urban rail extension to Rishikesh.

transcript.title: Around the world
transcript.text: And finally, here is what is happening around the world. Yemen's Houthis announce 'maritime embargo' on Saudi Arabia. The Houthis say the embargo begins immediately and is a response to a Saudi blockade of ports and airports in Yemen. Boy, 13, dies during World Cup celebrations in Spain. Two other young people were injured after a fountain partially collapsed in Salamanca's Ciudad Rodrigo. Hamas names Khalil al-Hayya as new overall leader. The Palestinian armed group's Qatar-based chief negotiator replaces Yahya Sinwar, who was killed by Israeli forces in Gaza in 2024. Scores feared dead after passenger ferry capsizes off Guyana. Sixty-seven people have been rescued but the ferry is thought to have had more than 130 people on board.

transcript.title: Sign-off
transcript.text: That is your briefing. Stay sharp, and have a strong day.
